WARNING: These test points are for A2442_CTO (820-02443) ONLY. Page numbers differ from base A2442 (820-02098). SVR AON is 42A design. Component designators are board-specific.
data_source: Adapted from base A2442 diagnostic data + CTO schematic analysis. No CTO-specific Saf data yet.

faults.name: PPBUS_AON cap short (CR765)
faults.root_cause: Capacitor short on PPBUS_AON distribution. No liquid damage in this case.
faults.fix: Remove CR765 (diode mode confirms short). Retest. If short clears, replace cap. If persists, trace downstream.

faults.name: CD3217 liquid damage (UF400/U5500)
faults.root_cause: Liquid ingress under CD3217 or level shifter ICs. Corrodes BGA pads.
faults.fix: Clean board thoroughly. Replace affected CD3217. Check level shifters (UF260, UF720). May need multi-IC replacement.

faults.name: Viper VR (U9300) failure — Pro/Max specific (3 stage pages CTO)
faults.root_cause: Viper VR controller dead or power stage failure. Board partially boots but PCPU domains don't power up. CTO has 3 power stage pages (49-51) vs base 2 — more phases, more potential failure points.
faults.fix: If VIPER_ALERT_L is low -> Viper has detected a fault. Check power stages (pages 49-51 CTO). If CZ610 has no output -> U9300 dead.
faults.cto_note: CTO has additional Viper Power Stages 3 (page 51) with extra phases for Max CPU clusters.

faults.name: Monaco VR failure — Pro/Max specific (5 instances CTO)
faults.root_cause: Monaco VR not starting. GPU/fabric domains not powered. CTO has 5 instances (C0-C4, pages 52-56) vs base 3.
faults.fix: Check each Monaco instance individually. CTO Max has instances C2 (page 54) and C3 (page 55) that are CTO-only. If all Monaco outputs dead -> controller IC fault. Single instance failure may leave partial GPU function.
faults.cto_note: Monaco 2 (C2, page 54) and Monaco 3 (C3, page 55) are CTO-only instances for Max GPU phases. These are additional failure points not present on base A2442.

faults.name: U5700 SVR AON failure (42A CTO design)
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all M-series boards
faults.root_cause: SVR AON controller dead, phase driver fault, or downstream short on PP3V8_AON pulling current. CTO 42A design has 3-phase summing amp ISENSE/ILIMIT (page 36). BOM thresholds: BOM0=30A, BOM1=35A, BOM2=42A.
faults.fix: If CZ400 = 0V -> check PPVAON_PWR_EN. If enable asserted but no output -> U5700 dead. If output shorted -> thermal cam on PP3V8_AON branches.
faults.cto_note: 42A design has higher current capacity than base 30A. More heat potential when short occurs.

faults.name: MPMU (U8100) failure
faults.affected_rail: All MPMU buck and LDO outputs
faults.root_cause: Dead MPMU, liquid damage on BGA, or downstream short loading outputs.
faults.test_points: PPU815-PPU836 (PMU test points page 135), MPMU output caps CR8xx (page 41)
faults.fix: If multiple outputs dead -> replace U8100. If single output dead -> find downstream short on that rail.

faults.name: Dual SSD / Ocarina fault
faults.root_cause: SSD0 or SSD1 NAND failure, Ocarina controller dead, or NAND power VR fault.
faults.fix: Check both SSD power rails independently. If one SSD's power is shorted -> isolate that SSD. Try DFU restore. If NAND VR shorted -> replace Ocarina.
faults.note: A2442 CTO has dual SSD (SSD0 + SSD1, 8 NAND packages total). Failure of one SSD may still allow partial boot.

faults.name: HDMI Madea controller fault
faults.fix: Board boots normally. HDMI output dead. Check Madea power rails, HDMI DDC I2C, HPD signal.

faults.name: SD card controller fault
faults.fix: Check PP3V3_AWAKE_SW_SD (power switch). Check SD_CD_L (card detect). Check controller IC.

faults.name: ROM / firmware corruption
faults.root_cause: ROM IC not getting 3.3V LDO power. Resistor fault on LDO line.
faults.fix: Check ROM LDO power path. Replace damaged resistors in LDO feedback circuit.
